sourceMovie.title: The Dressmaker
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Dressmaker works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hunt for the Wilderpeople
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Dressmaker clicked for you, Hunt for the Wilderpeople (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Dressmaker clicked for you, The Homesman (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Dressmaker?
faq.answer: Try Hunt for the Wilderpeople, The Homesman, First Cow—each shares DNA with The Dressmaker while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Dressmaker?
faq.answer: The Dressmaker (2015) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Dressmaker worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
